title: Calamus ochrolepis
intro: Calamus ochrolepis, also known as yellow root or sweet root, is a perennial plant species belonging to the family Zingiberaceae. It is valued for its potential health benefits, which include anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant properties that may help alleviate digestive issues, reduce pain, and promote oral health. The bioactive constituents of Calamus ochrolepis include sesquiterpenes, such as calamusin and isocalamusin, as well as other compounds like flavonoids and phenolic acids. Calamus ochrolepis is commonly used in herbal preparations, including tinctures, teas, and capsules, often in combination with other herbs, to support digestive health and address various health concerns.
taxonomy: Calamus ochrolepis belongs to the family Zingiberaceae, which is a large and diverse family of flowering plants commonly known as the ginger family. This family includes many well-known species such as ginger, turmeric, and cardamom. Calamus ochrolepis is a species of flowering plant that is native to Southeast Asia. It is a type of rhizomatous plant that grows in tropical and subtropical regions. The exact classification of Calamus ochrolepis may vary depending on the source, but it is generally classified as a member of the genus Calamus.
